rationale: The revised plan addresses both R4 hard blocks. Bead 4 now names the real executor seams: CompleteBead calls gitutil.MergeInto at mindspec_executor.go:291; FinalizeEpic calls gitutil.MergeInto at :370 for bead-to-spec auto-merge and gitutil.MergeBranch at :432 for the direct spec-to-main merge. Guarding those sites covers the in-repo bead-to-spec and direct spec-to-main merge mutations. Bead 3 now explicitly adds GitMv, ResetHard, CleanForce, LocalBranchRefs, and RemoteTrackingRefs across internal/gitutil, the Executor interface, MindspecExecutor, and MockExecutor; rg confirms these do not exist today. New citations are grounded: internal/spec.List, internal/domain.List, and internal/domain.Show exist; internal/doctor exists and links.go is correctly described as a net-new link-check lane. No material nonexistent seam remains.

findings.issue: The nonexistent internal/complete MergeBranch reference was removed. The plan now pins the guard to MindspecExecutor.CompleteBead and FinalizeEpic, directly before the real gitutil.MergeInto and gitutil.MergeBranch call sites.

The plan now lists the missing git mover primitives and executor surface as Bead 3 changed surfaces, including internal/gitutil/gitops.go, internal/executor/executor.go, MindspecExecutor, and MockExecutor. Those APIs are absent today, matching the net-new implementation claim.

internal/spec.List, internal/domain.List, and internal/domain.Show exist. internal/doctor/links.go does not exist yet, but the plan consistently describes it as a new link-check lane under the existing internal/doctor package.

FinalizeEpic's gitutil.MergeBranch site is the direct/no-remote spec-to-main path; when a remote exists FinalizeEpic pushes the branch for PR instead of performing a local merge. This is a wording caveat, not a blocker for the requested real-seam check.
